title: Binary search tree: comparisons bounded by height
statement_informal: Searching a binary search tree performs at most height(t) key comparisons (one per level on the search path). For a balanced tree this is O(log n) — the binary-search bound.

cost_model.notes: One key comparison per node on the root-to-node search path; bounded by the tree height.

references: Knuth, TAOCP Vol. 3 §6.2.2 — binary tree searching; §6.2.1 — binary search (O(log n)).
Corollary: a balanced BST on n keys has height O(log n).
